plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% for a stronger income component and stability.
Trim CRWD to 8% to manage single name concentration.
Initiate a new position in XLV at 5% to diversify into the healthcare sector.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 15-20% by reducing overall exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5% per month. It significantly reduces risk by cutting all leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration. Expect lower volatility, but also limited upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with the single name concentration limit.
Reduce SOXL to 2% and sell SPAL to lower overall leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to 15% to balance income with growth potential.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into a strong performing AI/Software name.
Initiate a new position in RTX at 5% to diversify with a high-scoring setup.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 10% to manage risk and provide flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% per month with a risk-first growth approach. It reduces leverage and concentration while blending broad index with quality tech. Expect moderate volatility and growth potential, with a manageable drawdown profile.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to adhere to the single name concentration limit.
Increase SOXL to 10% to lean into leveraged semiconductor exposure, aligned with aggressive growth.
Sell SPAL and QQQI to streamline the portfolio for higher growth potential and reduce income focus.
Increase CRWD to 20% to concentrate on a high-performing AI/Software name.
Initiate a new position in AMAT at 10% to add a high risk/reward semiconductor setup.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer of approximately 5% to maximize market exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month by stretching growth and leveraging high-scoring setups. It significantly increases exposure to leveraged ETFs and concentration, leading to higher upside but also much larger potential drawdowns. Daily decay and rebalancing of leveraged ETFs amplify risk; strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate while retaining broad market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, prioritizing stability.
Increase QQQI to 30% to maximize income generation through covered-call strategies.
Sell CRWD to reduce high-beta single name exposure, focusing on lower drawdown.
Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce growth-oriented exposure while maintaining some broad index.
Maintain a cash buffer to further stabilize the portfolio and provide liquidity for distributions.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% per month by prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta single names, rotating into income-focused ETFs. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in market downturns, making it distinct from a savings account.
